827	        return self.run(q, k, v, scale_q, scale_k, scale_v)
828	
829	    @flashinfer_api(trace=block_sparse_attention_run_trace)
830	    def run(
831	        self,
832	        q: torch.Tensor,
833	        k: torch.Tensor,
834	        v: torch.Tensor,
835	        scale_q: Optional[torch.Tensor] = None,
836	        scale_k: Optional[torch.Tensor] = None,
837	        scale_v: Optional[torch.Tensor] = None,
838	        out: Optional[torch.Tensor] = None,
839	        lse: Optional[torch.Tensor] = None,
840	        return_lse: bool = False,
841	        enable_pdl: Optional[bool] = None,
842	    ) -> Union[torch.Tensor, Tuple[torch.Tensor, torch.Tensor]]:
843	        r"""Compute block-sparse attention between Q/K/V tensors.
844	
845	        Parameters
846	        ----------
847	        q : torch.Tensor
848	            The query tensor with shape ``(M, num_qo_heads, head_dim)``.
849	        k : torch.Tensor
850	            The key tensor with shape ``(N, num_kv_heads, head_dim)``.
851	        v : torch.Tensor
852	            The value tensor with shape ``(N, num_kv_heads, head_dim)``.
853	        scale_q : Optional[torch.Tensor]
854	            The scale tensor for query, per-head quantization with shape: ``[num_qo_heads]``.
855	            Used with FP8 Quantization. If not provided, will be set to ``1.0``.
856	        scale_k : Optional[torch.Tensor]
857	            The scale tensor for key, per-head quantization with shape: ``[num_kv_heads]``.
858	            Used with FP8 Quantization. If not provided, will be set to ``1.0``.
859	        scale_v : Optional[torch.Tensor]
860	            The scale tensor for value, per-head quantization with shape: ``[num_kv_heads]``.
861	            Used with FP8 Quantization. If not provided, will be set to ``1.0``.
862	        out : Optional[torch.Tensor]
863	            The output tensor, if not provided, will be allocated internally.
864	        lse : Optional[torch.Tensor]
865	            The log-sum-exp of attention logits, if not provided, will be allocated internally.
866	        return_lse : bool
867	            Whether to return the log-sum-exp of attention logits
868	        enable_pdl : bool
869	            Whether to enable Programmatic Dependent Launch (PDL). See https://docs.nvidia.com/cuda/cuda-c-programming-guide/#programmatic-dependent-launch-and-synchronization
870	            Only supported for >= sm90, and currently only for FA2 and CUDA core decode.
871	
872	        Returns
873	        -------
874	        Union[torch.Tensor, Tuple[torch.Tensor, torch.Tensor]]
875	            If :attr:`return_lse` is ``False``, the attention output, shape: ``[M, num_qo_heads, head_dim]``.
876	            If :attr:`return_lse` is ``True``, a tuple of two tensors:
877	
878	            * The attention output, shape: ``[M, num_qo_heads, head_dim]``.
879	            * The logsumexp of attention output, shape: ``[M, num_qo_heads]``.
880	        """
881	        if enable_pdl is None:
882	            enable_pdl = device_support_pdl(q.device)
883	
884	        # ---- VSA Blackwell backend (BSA blk128 kernel) ----------------------------
885	        if self._backend == "vsa_blackwell":
886	            from flashinfer.cute_dsl.sparse import bsa_attn_fwd  # noqa: PLC0415
887	
888	            sm_scale = self._sm_scale
889	            if sm_scale is None:
890	                sm_scale = 1.0 / math.sqrt(q.size(-1))
891	
892	            # NHD -> BSHD  (batch=1, seqlen, heads, dim) — layout expected by BSA kernel
893	            Q_bsa = q.unsqueeze(0).contiguous()  # [1, M, H, D]
894	            K_bsa = k.unsqueeze(0).contiguous()  # [1, N, H_kv, D]
895	            V_bsa = v.unsqueeze(0).contiguous()  # [1, N, H_kv, D]
896	
897	            o_bsa, lse_bsa = bsa_attn_fwd(
898	                Q_bsa,
899	                K_bsa,
900	                V_bsa,
901	                self._vsa_q2k_index,  # [1, H, MB, NB] int32
902	                block_sparse_num=2,  # ignored when q2k_block_nums is provided
903	                block_sizes=None,  # full blocks (no per-block padding masking)
904	                q2k_block_nums=self._vsa_q2k_num,  # [1, H, MB] int32
905	                softmax_scale=sm_scale,
906	                return_lse=True,
907	            )
908	
909	            # BSHD -> NHD: [1, M, H, D][0] -> [M, H, D]
910	            output = o_bsa[0]
911	            if out is not None:
912	                out.copy_(output)
913	                output = out
914	
915	            if return_lse:
916	                # [1, H, M] -> [M, H]
917	                lse_out = lse_bsa[0].permute(1, 0).contiguous()
918	                if lse is not None:
919	                    lse.copy_(lse_out)
920	                    lse_out = lse
921	                return output, lse_out
922	            return output
923	
924	        # ---- VSA Blackwell blk64 backend (BSA blk64 C++ kernel) ------------------
925	        if self._backend == "vsa_blackwell_blk64":
926	            from flashinfer.cute_dsl.sparse import bsa_attn_blk64_fwd  # noqa: PLC0415
927	
928	            sm_scale = self._sm_scale
929	            if sm_scale is None:
930	                sm_scale = 1.0 / math.sqrt(q.size(-1))
931	
932	            # NHD -> BSHD  (batch=1, seqlen, heads, dim)
933	            Q_bsa = q.unsqueeze(0).contiguous()
934	            K_bsa = k.unsqueeze(0).contiguous()
935	            V_bsa = v.unsqueeze(0).contiguous()
936	
937	            o_bsa, lse_bsa = bsa_attn_blk64_fwd(
938	                Q_bsa,
939	                K_bsa,
940	                V_bsa,
941	                self._vsa_q2k_index,
942	                block_sparse_num=1,  # ignored when q2k_block_nums is provided
943	                block_sizes=None,
944	                q2k_block_nums=self._vsa_q2k_num,
945	                softmax_scale=sm_scale,
946	                return_lse=True,
947	            )
948	
949	            # BSHD -> NHD
950	            output = o_bsa[0]
951	            if out is not None:
952	                out.copy_(output)
953	                output = out
954	
955	            if return_lse:
956	                # lse: (1, H, M) -> (M, H)
957	                lse_out = lse_bsa[0].permute(1, 0).contiguous()
958	                if lse is not None:
959	                    lse.copy_(lse_out)
960	                    lse_out = lse
961	                return output, lse_out
962	            return output
963	
964	        pos_encoding_mode = self._pos_encoding_mode
965	        logits_soft_cap = self._logits_soft_cap
966	        sm_scale = self._sm_scale
967	        rope_scale = self._rope_scale
968	        rope_theta = self._rope_theta
969	        _check_pos_encoding_mode(pos_encoding_mode)
970	        if logits_soft_cap is None:
971	            logits_soft_cap = 0.0
972	        if sm_scale is None:
973	            sm_scale = 1.0 / math.sqrt(q.size(-1))
974	        if rope_scale is None:
975	            rope_scale = 1.0
976	        if rope_theta is None:
977	            rope_theta = 1e4
978	        k = k.reshape(-1, self.C, *k.shape[-2:])
979	        v = v.reshape(-1, self.C, *v.shape[-2:])
980	
981	        if return_lse:
982	            if lse is None:
983	                lse = torch.empty(
984	                    (q.size(0), q.size(1)), dtype=torch.float32, device=q.device
985	                )
986	            else:
987	                check_shape_dtype_device(
988	                    lse, (q.size(0), q.size(1)), torch.float32, q.device, "lse"
989	                )
990	
991	        if out is None:
992	            out = torch.empty_like(q, dtype=self._o_dtype)
993	        else:
994	            check_shape_dtype_device(out, q.shape, self._o_dtype, q.device, "out")
995	
996	        if is_float8(q):
997	            assert q.dtype == k.dtype == v.dtype
998	            assert q.shape[-1] == k.shape[-1] == v.shape[-1]
999	            assert self._backend == "fa3" and self._use_tensor_cores
1000	
1001	            if scale_q is None:
1002	                scale_q = torch.ones(q.shape[1], dtype=torch.float32, device=q.device)
1003	            if scale_k is None:
1004	                scale_k = torch.ones(k.shape[1], dtype=torch.float32, device=q.device)
1005	            if scale_v is None:
1006	                scale_v = torch.ones(v.shape[1], dtype=torch.float32, device=q.device)
1007	
1008	        if self._use_tensor_cores:

1074	class VariableBlockSparseAttentionWrapper:
1075	    r"""Wrapper class for attention computation with a block-sparse matrix as attention mask.
1076	    This API supports variable block sizes provided by ``block_row_sz`` and ``block_col_sz``.
1077	    Besides, each ``kv_head_idx`` can specify its own sparse patterns without using the same mask.
1078	
1079	    Example
1080	    -------
1081	    >>> import torch
1082	    >>> import flashinfer
1083	    >>> num_qo_heads = 1
1084	    >>> num_kv_heads = 1
1085	    >>> head_dim = 128
1086	    >>> seq_len = 6 # This corresponds to the `block_row_sz` and `block_col_sz`
1087	    >>> # allocate 128MB workspace buffer
1088	    >>> workspace_buffer = torch.empty(128 * 1024 * 1024, dtype=torch.uint8, device="cuda:0")
1089	    >>> wrapper = flashinfer.VariableBlockSparseAttentionWrapper(workspace_buffer)
1090	    >>> block_mask_map = torch.tensor([[[0, 0, 1], [1, 0, 1], [0, 1, 1]]], dtype=torch.bool, device="cuda:0")
1091	    >>> block_row_sz = torch.tensor([[1, 2, 3]], dtype=torch.int32, device="cuda:0")
1092	    >>> block_col_sz = torch.tensor([[3, 1, 2]], dtype=torch.int32, device="cuda:0")
1093	    >>> wrapper.plan(
1094	    ...     block_mask_map,
1095	    ...     block_row_sz,
1096	    ...     block_col_sz,
1097	    ...     num_qo_heads,
1098	    ...     num_kv_heads,
1099	    ...     head_dim,
1100	    ... )
1101	    >>> q = torch.randn((num_qo_heads, seq_len, head_dim), dtype=torch.float16, device="cuda:0")
1102	    >>> k = torch.randn((num_kv_heads, seq_len, head_dim), dtype=torch.float16, device="cuda:0")
1103	    >>> v = torch.randn((num_kv_heads, seq_len, head_dim), dtype=torch.float16, device="cuda:0")
1104	    >>> o = wrapper.run(q, k, v)
1105	    """
1106	
1107	    @flashinfer_api
1108	    def __init__(
1109	        self,
1110	        float_workspace_buffer: torch.Tensor,
1111	        backend: str = "auto",
1112	    ) -> None:
1113	        r"""Constructs of :class:`VariableBlockSparseAttentionWrapper`.
1114	
1115	        Parameters
1116	        ----------
1117	        float_workspace_buffer : torch.Tensor
1118	            The user reserved float workspace buffer used to store intermediate attention results
1119	            in the split-k algorithm. The recommended size is 128MB, the device of the workspace
1120	            buffer should be the same as the device of the input tensors.
1121	        backend : str
1122	            The implementation backend, could be ``auto``/``fa2`` or ``fa3``. Defaults to ``auto``.
1123	            If set to ``auto``, the function will automatically choose the backend based on the
1124	            device architecture and kernel availability.
1125	        """
1126	        self._float_workspace_buffer = float_workspace_buffer
1127	        self.device = float_workspace_buffer.device
1128	        self._workspace_size = (
1129	            float_workspace_buffer.numel() * float_workspace_buffer.element_size()
1130	        )
1131	        self._int_workspace_buffer = torch.empty(
1132	            (8 * 1024 * 1024,), dtype=torch.uint8, device=self.device
1133	        )
1134	
1135	        self._kv_lens_buffer = torch.empty(
1136	            (32768,), dtype=torch.int32, device=self.device
1137	        )
1138	        self._pin_memory_int_workspace_buffer = torch.empty(
1139	            self._int_workspace_buffer.shape,
1140	            dtype=torch.uint8,
1141	            pin_memory=True,
1142	            device="cpu",
1143	        )
1144	        self._use_cuda_graph = False
1145	        self._kv_layout = "NHD"
1146	        self._qo_indptr: Optional[torch.Tensor] = None
1147	        self._paged_kv_indptr_buf: Optional[torch.Tensor] = None
1148	        self._paged_kv_indices_buf: Optional[torch.Tensor] = None
1149	        self._paged_kv_last_page_len: Optional[torch.Tensor] = None
1150	        self._backend = backend
1151	
1152	    def reset_workspace_buffer(
1153	        self,
1154	        float_workspace_buffer: torch.Tensor,
1155	        int_workspace_buffer: torch.Tensor,
1156	    ) -> None:
1157	        r"""Reset the workspace buffer.
1158	
1159	        Parameters
1160	        ----------
1161	        float_workspace_buffer : torch.Tensor
1162	            The new float workspace buffer, the device of the new float workspace buffer should
1163	            be the same as the device of the input tensors.
1164	
1165	        int_workspace_buffer : torch.Tensor
1166	            The new int workspace buffer, the device of the new int workspace buffer should
1167	            be the same as the device of the input tensors.
1168	        """
1169	        self._float_workspace_buffer = float_workspace_buffer
1170	        self._int_workspace_buffer = int_workspace_buffer
1171	        self._workspace_size = (
1172	            float_workspace_buffer.numel() * float_workspace_buffer.element_size()
1173	        )
1174	        self._pin_memory_int_workspace_buffer = torch.empty(
1175	            self._int_workspace_buffer.shape,
1176	            dtype=self._int_workspace_buffer.dtype,
1177	            pin_memory=True,
1178	        )

1180	    @flashinfer_api
1181	    def plan(
1182	        self,
1183	        block_mask_map: torch.Tensor,
1184	        block_row_sz: torch.Tensor,
1185	        block_col_sz: torch.Tensor,
1186	        num_qo_heads: int,
1187	        num_kv_heads: int,
1188	        head_dim: int,
1189	        causal: bool = False,
1190	        pos_encoding_mode: str = "NONE",
1191	        use_fp16_qk_reduction: bool = False,
1192	        logits_soft_cap: Optional[float] = None,
1193	        sm_scale: Optional[float] = None,
1194	        rope_scale: Optional[float] = None,
1195	        rope_theta: Optional[float] = None,
1196	        non_blocking: bool = True,
1197	        q_data_type: Union[str, torch.dtype] = "float16",
1198	        kv_data_type: Optional[Union[str, torch.dtype]] = None,
1199	    ) -> None:
1200	        r"""Create auxiliary data structures for block sparse attention.
1201	
1202	        Parameters
1203	        ----------
1204	        block_mask_map : torch.Tensor
1205	            The block mask map (boolean), shape ``(num_kv_heads, MB, NB)``, where ``MB`` is the number of blocks in the row dimension,
1206	            ``NB`` is the number of blocks in the column dimension.
1207	        block_row_sz : torch.Tensor
1208	            The block row size, shape ``(num_kv_heads, MB,)``.
1209	        block_col_sz : torch.Tensor
1210	            The block column size, shape ``(num_kv_heads, NB,)``.
1211	        num_qo_heads : int
1212	            The number of heads in the query/output tensor.
1213	        num_kv_heads : int
1214	            The number of heads in the key/value tensor. Note that a group of ``qo_heads`` shares the same sparse pattern of ``kv_heads``.
1215	        head_dim : int
1216	            The dimension of each head.
1217	        causal : bool
1218	            Whether to apply causal mask to the attention matrix.
1219	        pos_encoding_mode : str, optional
1220	            The position encoding applied inside attention kernels, could be
1221	            ``NONE``/``ROPE_LLAMA`` (LLAMA style rotary embedding) /``ALIBI``.
1222	            Default is ``NONE``.
1223	        use_fp16_qk_reduction : bool
1224	            Whether to use f16 for qk reduction (faster at the cost of slight precision
1225	            loss).
1226	        logits_soft_cap : Optional[float]
1227	            The attention logits soft capping value (used in Gemini, Grok and Gemma-2, etc.), if not
1228	            provided, will be set to ``0``. If greater than 0, the logits will be capped according to
1229	            formula:
1230	            :math:`\texttt{logits_soft_cap} \times \mathrm{tanh}(x / \texttt{logits_soft_cap})`,
1231	            where :math:`x` is the input logits.
1232	        sm_scale : Optional[float]
1233	            The scale used in softmax, if not provided, will be set to
1234	            ``1.0 / sqrt(head_dim)``.
1235	        rope_scale : Optional[float]

1237	            ``1.0``.
1238	        rope_theta : Optional[float]
1239	            The theta used in RoPE, if not provided, will be set to ``1e4``.
1240	        non_blocking : bool
1241	            Whether to copy the input tensors to the device asynchronously, defaults to ``True``.
1242	        q_data_type : Union[str, torch.dtype]
1243	            Dtype of the query tensor.  Used to specialize the JIT-compiled kernel.
1244	            Defaults to ``"float16"``.
1245	        kv_data_type : Optional[Union[str, torch.dtype]]
1246	            Dtype of the key/value tensors.  When ``None``, defaults to
1247	            ``q_data_type``.
1248	
1249	
1250	        The :meth:`plan` method should be called before any :meth:`run` or
1251	        :meth:`run_return_lse` calls, auxiliary data structures will be created
1252	        during this call and cached for multiple kernel runs.
1253	
1254	        The ``num_qo_heads`` must be a multiple of ``num_kv_heads``. If ``num_qo_heads``
1255	        is not equal to ``num_kv_heads``, the function will use
1256	        `grouped query attention <https://arxiv.org/abs/2305.13245>`_.
1257	        """
1258	        q_data_type = canonicalize_torch_dtype(q_data_type)
1259	        if kv_data_type is None:
1260	            kv_data_type = q_data_type
1261	        kv_data_type = canonicalize_torch_dtype(kv_data_type)
1262	        self._o_dtype = q_data_type
1263	
1264	        if logits_soft_cap is None:
1265	            logits_soft_cap = 0.0
1266	
1267	        # num_blocks are constant across kv_heads
1268	        num_blocks_row = block_row_sz.shape[-1]
1269	        num_blocks_col = block_col_sz.shape[-1]
1270	
1271	        # q layout: [seq_len, num_kv_heads, gqa_group_size, head_dim]
1272	        # padded into: [seq_len * num_kv_heads, 1, gqa_group_size, head_dim]
1273	        qo_indptr = torch.cat(
1274	            [
1275	                torch.zeros(1, dtype=torch.int32, device=block_row_sz.device),
1276	                torch.cumsum(block_row_sz.flatten(), dim=0, dtype=torch.int32),
1277	            ],
1278	            dim=0,
1279	        )
1280	        qo_indptr_host = qo_indptr.to("cpu", non_blocking=non_blocking)
1281	        last_block_len = torch.full(
1282	            (num_blocks_row * num_kv_heads,),
1283	            1,
1284	            dtype=torch.int32,
1285	            device=block_mask_map.device,
1286	        )  # We use page_size == 1 for variable length support
1287	
1288	        # HND kv layout: [num_kv_heads, num_blocks, block_size, head_dim]
1289	        # padded into: [num_kv_heads * num_blocks, block_size, 1, head_dim]
1290	        # for customized attention mask for each kv_head
1291	        # NOTE(Yilong): This could be perf bottleneck. Consider Triton implementation.
1292	        def _block_mask_map_to_expanded_indices(
1293	            block_mask_map: torch.Tensor,  # [H, R, C] bool / {0,1}
1294	            block_col_sz: torch.Tensor,  # [H, C]     int
1295	        ) -> Tuple[torch.Tensor, torch.Tensor]:
1296	            """
1297	            Args:
1298	                block_mask_map:  bool/int  [num_kv_heads, num_blocks_row, num_blocks_col]
1299	                block_col_sz:    int32/64  [num_kv_heads, num_blocks_col]
1300	            Returns:
1301	                kv_indptr:  [H*R + 1]  int32  —  CSR indptr
1302	                kv_indices: [nnz]      int32  —  token indices per (head, row)
1303	            """
1304	            device = block_mask_map.device
1305	            dtype_i = torch.int32
1306	
1307	            # 1) Calculate the total length of each row (head, row)
1308	            row_lengths = (block_mask_map * block_col_sz[:, None, :]).sum(-1)  # [H,R]
1309	            kv_indptr = torch.cat(
1310	                [
1311	                    torch.zeros(1, dtype=dtype_i, device=device),
1312	                    torch.cumsum(row_lengths.flatten(), 0),
1313	                ],
1314	                dim=0,
1315	            )
1316	
1317	            # 2) Calculate the offset of each column block within its head

1319	                torch.cumsum(block_col_sz.to(dtype_i), 1) - block_col_sz
1320	            )  # [H,C]
1321	            head_len = block_col_sz.sum(1, dtype=dtype_i)
1322	            head_offset = torch.cumsum(head_len, 0) - head_len
1323	
1324	            # 3) Find all selected (h,r,c)
1325	            h_idx, r_idx, c_idx = block_mask_map.nonzero(as_tuple=True)

1327	            base = head_offset[h_idx] + col_offset[h_idx, c_idx]  # [N]
1328	
1329	            # 4) Expand variable-length column blocks into token-level indices
1330	            cum = torch.cumsum(lengths, 0)

1332	            offsets_within = torch.arange(cum[-1], device=device) - starts
1333	            kv_indices = torch.repeat_interleave(base, lengths) + offsets_within
1334	
1335	            return kv_indptr.to(dtype=dtype_i, device=device), kv_indices.to(
1336	                dtype=dtype_i, device=device
1337	            )
1338	
1339	        kv_indptr, kv_indices = _block_mask_map_to_expanded_indices(
1340	            block_mask_map, block_col_sz
1341	        )
1342	        kv_indptr_host = kv_indptr.to("cpu", non_blocking=non_blocking)

1463	    def run(

1465	        q: torch.Tensor,

1471	        enable_pdl: Optional[bool] = None,

1473	        r"""Compute block-sparse attention between Q/K/V tensors.
1474	
1475	        Parameters
1476	        ----------
1477	        q : torch.Tensor
1478	            The query tensor with shape ``(num_qo_heads, qo_len, head_dim)``.
1479	        k : torch.Tensor

1481	        v : torch.Tensor

1494	        -------
1495	        Union[torch.Tensor, Tuple[torch.Tensor, torch.Tensor]]
1496	            If :attr:`return_lse` is ``False``, the attention output, shape: ``[M, num_qo_heads, head_dim]``.
1497	            If :attr:`return_lse` is ``True``, a tuple of two tensors:
1498	
1499	            * The attention output, shape: ``[M, num_qo_heads, head_dim]``.
1500	            * The logsumexp of attention output, shape: ``[M, num_qo_heads]``.
1501	        """
1502	        # NOTE(Zihao): defer import of einops
1503	        import einops
1504	
1505	        if enable_pdl is None:
1506	            enable_pdl = device_support_pdl(q.device)
1507	
1508	        pos_encoding_mode = self._pos_encoding_mode
1509	        logits_soft_cap = self._logits_soft_cap
1510	        sm_scale = self._sm_scale
1511	        rope_scale = self._rope_scale
1512	        rope_theta = self._rope_theta
1513	        _check_pos_encoding_mode(pos_encoding_mode)
1514	        if logits_soft_cap is None:
1515	            logits_soft_cap = 0.0
1516	        if sm_scale is None:
1517	            sm_scale = 1.0 / math.sqrt(q.size(-1))
1518	        if rope_scale is None:
1519	            rope_scale = 1.0
1520	        if rope_theta is None:
1521	            rope_theta = 1e4
1522	
1523	        # reshape to pad num_kv_heads into seq_len
1524	        # input [num_qo_heads, qo_len, head_dim]
1525	        # kernel layout is NHD -> [qo_len * num_kv_heads, gqa_group_size, head_dim]
1526	        q = einops.rearrange(
1527	            q,
1528	            "(num_kv_heads gqa_group_size) qo_len head_dim -> (num_kv_heads qo_len) gqa_group_size head_dim",
1529	            num_kv_heads=self._num_kv_heads,
1530	        ).contiguous()
1531	        # HND -> [kv_len * num_kv_heads (num_pages), 1 (page_size), 1 (new_num_kv_heads), head_dim]
1532	        k = einops.rearrange(
1533	            k,
1534	            "num_kv_heads kv_len head_dim -> (num_kv_heads kv_len) 1 1 head_dim",
1535	        ).contiguous()
1536	        v = einops.rearrange(
1537	            v,
1538	            "num_kv_heads kv_len head_dim -> (num_kv_heads kv_len) 1 1 head_dim",
1539	        ).contiguous()
1540	
1541	        if return_lse:
1542	            if lse is None:
1543	                lse = torch.empty(
1544	                    (q.size(0), q.size(1)), dtype=torch.float32, device=q.device
1545	                )
1546	            else:
1547	                check_shape_dtype_device(
1548	                    lse, (q.size(0), q.size(1)), torch.float32, q.device, "lse"
1549	                )
1550	
1551	        if out is None:
1552	            out = torch.empty_like(q, dtype=self._o_dtype)
1553	        else:
1554	            check_shape_dtype_device(out, q.shape, self._o_dtype, q.device, "out")
1555	
1556	        self._cached_module.paged_run(
1557	            self._float_workspace_buffer,
1558	            self._int_workspace_buffer,
1559	            self._plan_info,
1560	            q,
1561	            k,
1562	            v,
1563	            self._qo_indptr,
1564	            self._paged_kv_indptr_buf,
1565	            self._paged_kv_indices_buf,
1566	            self._paged_kv_last_page_len,
1567	            out,
1568	            lse,
1569	            self._mask_mode,
1570	            TensorLayout[self._kv_layout].value,
1571	            -1,  # window_left
1572	            enable_pdl,
1573	            # ADDITIONAL_FUNC_PARAMS
1574	            # Not supported yet
1575	            None,  # packed_mask_buf
1576	            None,  # mask_indptr_buf
1577	            None,  # alibi_slopes_buf
1578	            None,
1579	            None,
1580	            None,
1581	            logits_soft_cap,
1582	            sm_scale,
1583	            None,  # scale_q
1584	            None,  # scale_k
1585	            None,  # scale_v
1586	            rope_scale,
1587	            rope_theta,
1588	            0,  # token_pos_in_items_len
1589	            self._workspace_size,
1590	        )
1591	
1592	        # [qo_len * num_kv_heads, gqa_group_size, head_dim] -> HND
1593	        out = einops.rearrange(
1594	            out,
1595	            "(num_kv_heads qo_len) gqa_group_size head_dim -> (num_kv_heads gqa_group_size) qo_len head_dim",
1596	            num_kv_heads=self._num_kv_heads,
1597	        ).contiguous()
1598	
1599	        if return_lse:
1600	            lse = einops.rearrange(
1601	                lse,
1602	                "(num_kv_heads qo_len) gqa_group_size -> (num_kv_heads gqa_group_size) qo_len",
1603	                num_kv_heads=self._num_kv_heads,
1604	            ).contiguous()
1605	
1606	        return (out, lse) if return_lse else out
1607

"""
